questions.question: According to the material, what best describes recursive self-improvement in AI today?
options: AI systems are fully autonomous with no human role left
Each model contributes to building later models, with humans still partly in the loop
Only hardware improvements matter; software does not recursively improve
Recursive improvement happens only in robotics, not in language models
questions.answer: Each model contributes to building later models, with humans still partly in the loop

questions.question: Why does the interview frame energy as central to future intelligence?
options: Because intelligence growth is tied to how much usable power can be harnessed
Because energy markets are easier to regulate than AI labs
Because only fossil fuels can support superintelligence
Because intelligence is measured by electricity price alone
questions.answer: Because intelligence growth is tied to how much usable power can be harnessed

questions.question: What is the main reason Musk gives for believing a hard takeoff is already happening?
options: Governments have paused AI safety regulation
Robotics has replaced all factory workers
Space manufacturing has reached full automation
Major AI breakthroughs are arriving at a pace that is difficult to track
questions.answer: Major AI breakthroughs are arriving at a pace that is difficult to track

questions.question: How is AI progress characterized in the outline rather than as a single endless exponential curve?
options: As overlapping S-curves with bursts and plateaus
As a strictly linear trend with no breakthroughs
As random jumps with no recognizable pattern
As a one-time discontinuity followed by stagnation
questions.answer: As overlapping S-curves with bursts and plateaus

questions.question: Which statement best captures the 10-year macroeconomic forecast presented in the discussion?
options: The economy is expected to shrink due to labor displacement
A roughly 10x economy is considered plausible absent major geopolitical disruption
Growth is expected to stay near historical averages only
Economic output is expected to stop mattering once AGI appears
questions.answer: A roughly 10x economy is considered plausible absent major geopolitical disruption

questions.question: What production timeline is described for Optimus 3?
options: Mass production starts immediately with no low-volume phase
Low-volume start in summer 2026, high-volume around summer 2027
Prototype only through 2030 with no planned scaling
Commercial release is canceled in favor of software-only AI
questions.answer: Low-volume start in summer 2026, high-volume around summer 2027

questions.question: How does the material describe Tesla's workforce strategy in relation to robotics?
options: Headcount is expected to decline sharply as robots replace humans
No meaningful change is expected in productivity per worker
The strategy is to avoid layoffs and increase output per human substantially
Human labor will be eliminated before Optimus reaches volume production
questions.answer: The strategy is to avoid layoffs and increase output per human substantially

questions.question: In the outline's economic argument, what drives persistent deflation?
options: Central banks permanently freezing money supply growth
Goods and services growing faster than the money supply
A complete ban on private companies
Declining energy availability in industrial systems
questions.answer: Goods and services growing faster than the money supply

questions.question: What does 'universal high income' mean in the context of the interview?
options: A temporary stimulus policy during recessions
A minimal guaranteed income designed only for unemployment
A tax incentive for high-tech companies
A broad abundance condition where material access rises well beyond subsistence
questions.answer: A broad abundance condition where material access rises well beyond subsistence

questions.question: Why does the discussion suggest money may become less relevant in the long run?
options: Because legal systems will ban all digital payment tools
Because production could eventually exceed aggregate human demand in many domains
Because inflation will make all currencies unusable within two years
Because companies will stop producing goods and switch to services only
questions.answer: Because production could eventually exceed aggregate human demand in many domains

questions.question: How is the 'singularity' used in the governance discussion?
options: As proof that policy institutions no longer matter
As a metaphor for why outcomes become harder to predict under rapid change
As evidence that AI progress will slow to zero
As a guarantee that all social outcomes will be positive
questions.answer: As a metaphor for why outcomes become harder to predict under rapid change

questions.question: What is the strongest healthcare claim associated with advanced AI and robotics in the outline?
options: Medical capability could become broadly accessible at levels exceeding what today's richest individuals receive
Healthcare quality will remain unchanged while only costs increase
AI is expected to replace all physicians immediately
Longevity research is considered unrelated to robotics
questions.answer: Medical capability could become broadly accessible at levels exceeding what today's richest individuals receive
